summary: Hidden Windows persistence and respawning supervisor on package load, bundled with FiveM/GTA V cheat native module
details: On require()/load (and via the package's bin entry), launcher.js installs a persistent autostart entry under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run named 'OneDriveUpdate' (spoofing Microsoft OneDrive) pointing at a shipped wscript-launched VBS dropper (OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs) that re-launches node.exe launcher.js hidden. The same launcher spawns itself as a detached, windowsHide:true child process with process.title='Runtime Broker' (impersonating the legitimate Windows system process) under SENSIVITY_SUPERVISOR=1, which continuously respawns a worker. The worker reads a 149KB heavily obfuscated sibling (server.obf.js, obfuscator.io with RC4-decoded ~724-entry string array) and passes it to eval(); the obfuscated module loads sens.node, a 6.5MB Windows x64 PE containing hardcoded GTA V / FiveM cheat strings ('Enable FL', 'Freecam', 'Teleport', 'Hospital', 'PinkMote', 'savePeekPosition', getFiveMProcessName scanning for '*GTAPr...' processes) and exposes performAction/getPlayerList/getVehicleList/setConfig over a local Socket.IO panel on port 3000. launcher.js also periodically writes a.yt.ps1 PowerShell script that uses UIAutomationClient to enumerate URL bars of all running Chrome/Edge/Opera/Brave windows looking for YouTube video id 'wJWta2lO0Lw'. None of this is documented, opt-in, or removable through normal uninstall. Installer harm: anyone who imports this package on Windows (typo, transitive dep, npx invocation) gets a hidden, auto-restarting background agent registered under a Microsoft-spoofing Run-key name, an opaque 6.5MB native binary that scans and manipulates other processes (game-cheat anti-TOS surface), and silent enumeration of their browsers' URL bars.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
